_meta.description: INVERTED ratio of reference cooling intensity to target cooling intensity. Unlike other compliance ratios, LOWER cooling intensity is better, so the formula is inverted. Values >= 100% indicate the target has equal or better (lower) cooling intensity than reference.

inputs.name: isReferenceCalculation
inputs.description: True when calculating for Reference model

preconditions.description: Target intensity must be positive for ratio calculation

behavior.note: INVERTED: Lower target intensity = higher (better) ratio

rationale.whyInverted: For cooling intensity, lower values are better (less cooling needed). Standard ratio (target/ref) would show low % for good buildings. Inverting (ref/target) makes higher % = better, matching other compliance metrics.
rationale.contrastWithOthers: AFUE, COPc, SRE use target/ref because higher values are better. Cooling intensity uses ref/target because lower values are better.

tests.name: Target better than reference (lower intensity)
tests.note: Target uses 33% less cooling energy, so compliance is 150%

tests.name: Target worse than reference
tests.note: Target uses 33% more cooling energy, so compliance is only 75%
